meta.name: ProQOL Professional Quality of Life Scale
meta.fullName: Professional Quality of Life Scale Version 5
meta.description: The ProQOL is a 30-item scale measuring compassion satisfaction, burnout, and secondary traumatic stress in helping professionals.
meta.author: Stamm BH (2010)
meta.reference: Stamm BH. The Concise ProQOL Manual. 2nd ed. Pocatello, ID: ProQOL.org; 2010.
meta.instruction: The following questions are about your experiences as a helper. Consider each item about you and your current work situation during the last 30 days.

questions.text: I am happy.
I am preoccupied with more than one person I help.
I get satisfaction from being able to help people.
I feel connected to others.
I jump or am startled by unexpected sounds.
I feel invigorated after working with those I help.
I find it difficult to separate my personal life from my life as a helper.
I am not as productive at work because I am losing sleep over traumatic experiences of a person I help.
I think that I might have been affected by the traumatic stress of those I help.
I feel trapped by my job as a helper.
Because of my helping, I have felt "on edge" about various things.
I like my work as a helper.
I feel depressed because of the traumatic experiences of the people I help.
I feel as though I am experiencing the trauma of someone I have helped.
I have beliefs that sustain me.
I am pleased with how I am able to keep up with helping techniques and protocols.
I am the person I always wanted to be.
My work makes me feel satisfied.
I feel worn out because of my work as a helper.
I have happy thoughts and feelings about those I help and how I could help them.
I feel overwhelmed because my case work load seems endless.
I believe I can make a difference through my work.
I avoid certain activities or situations because they remind me of frightening experiences of the people I help.
I am proud of what I can do to help.
As a result of my helping, I have intrusive, frightening thoughts.
I feel "bogged down" by the system.
I have thoughts that I am a "success" as a helper.
I can't recall important parts of my work with trauma victims.
I am a very caring person.
I am happy that I chose to do this work.

compassion_satisfaction.label: Low
compassion_satisfaction.description: Low compassion satisfaction. You may not feel fulfilled by your helping work. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

compassion_satisfaction.label: Moderate
compassion_satisfaction.description: Moderate compassion satisfaction. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care
Consider reducing unnecessary commitments and responsibilities
Ensure adequate sleep time

compassion_satisfaction.label: High
compassion_satisfaction.description: High compassion satisfaction. You gain significant positive rewards from helping. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

burnout.label: Low
burnout.description: Low burnout levels. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

burnout.label: Moderate
burnout.description: Moderate burnout. Consider stress management strategies. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care
Consider reducing unnecessary commitments and responsibilities
Ensure adequate sleep time

burnout.label: High
burnout.description: High burnout levels. Seek professional support. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

secondary_trauma.label: Low
secondary_trauma.description: Low secondary traumatic stress. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

secondary_trauma.label: Moderate
secondary_trauma.description: Moderate secondary traumatic stress. At this level, stress may cause persistent tension, mood fluctuations, and reduced work efficiency or rest quality.
suggestions: Consider discussing stress management strategies with a counselor
Evaluate and adjust major stressors in your work or personal life
Build daily habits of relaxation and self-care
Consider reducing unnecessary commitments and responsibilities
Ensure adequate sleep time

secondary_trauma.label: High
secondary_trauma.description: High secondary traumatic stress. Seek immediate support. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities
